ucs.title: [LLM] Fox Kitten (Pioneer Kitten/Lemon Sandstorm) edge-VPN exploitation followed by web shell drop on Pulse/Citrix/Fortinet/PAN appliances
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature initial-access chain: exploitation of internet-facing VPN/SSL gateways (CVE-2019-11510 Pulse Secure, CVE-2019-19781 Citrix ADC, CVE-2018-13379 Fortinet FortiOS, CVE-2024-24919 Check Point, CVE-2024-3400 PAN GlobalProtect) followed by ASPX/JSP/PHP web shells written under web roots and spawned by the appliance/web service identity. This chain is the defining Fox Kitten / UNC757 tradecraft documented by ClearSky, CISA AA20-259A, AA24-241A, and Mandiant.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","dsserver","nsppe","sslvpnd")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","klist.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as web_shell_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as web_shell_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.aspx","*.ashx","*.asmx","*.jsp","*.jspx","*.war","*.php") AND Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\*","*\\webapps\\*","*/htdocs/*","*/dana-na/*","*/vpns/*","*/global-protect/*") by Filesystem.dest] | where isnotnull(web_shell_path) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let appliance_or_web_proc = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","dsserver","nsppe","sslvpnd","cgi-bin"]);
let recon_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","klist.exe","reg.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","sc.exe"]);
let shell_paths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\",@"\webapps\","/htdocs/","/dana-na/","/vpns/","/global-protect/","/_vti_bin/"]);
let shell_ext = dynamic([".aspx",".ashx",".asmx",".jsp",".jspx",".war",".php"]);
let ShellDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where FileName has_any (shell_ext)
| where FolderPath has_any (shell_paths)
| project ShellTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ShellPath=FolderPath, ShellName=FileName, ShellInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName, ShellSHA256=SHA256;
let ApplianceSpawns = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (appliance_or_web_proc)
| where FileName in~ (recon_children)
| project SpawnTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, CmdLine=ProcessCommandLine;
ApplianceSpawns
| join kind=inner ShellDrops on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', SpawnTime, ShellTime)) <= 60
| extend FoxKittenIndicator = case(
    ParentProc has_any ("dsserver","nsppe"), "Pulse Secure (CVE-2019-11510)",
    ParentProc has_any ("sslvpnd"), "Fortinet (CVE-2018-13379)",
    ParentProc has "java.exe" and ShellPath has "vpns", "Citrix ADC (CVE-2019-19781)",
    ParentProc has "java.exe" and ShellPath has "global-protect", "PAN GlobalProtect (CVE-2024-3400)",
    "Generic web-svc shell drop")
| project SpawnTime, DeviceName, FoxKittenIndicator, ParentProc, ChildProc, CmdLine, ShellPath, ShellName, ShellSHA256
| sort by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: Fox Kitten/Pioneer Kitten/UNC757 is the most prolific Iranian operator targeting edge appliances and is named in CISA AA20-259A and AA24-241A specifically for chaining VPN CVEs to web shells (e.g., METIS/HiveJack-style ASPX, antsword/STEADYPULSE on Pulse, NOTROBIN-adjacent on Citrix). The detection joins a child shell/recon process spawning under the appliance service identity with a same-host web

ucs.title: [LLM] Fox Kitten ngrok / SSH reverse tunnel and frpc/chisel persistence with local account creation
ucs.description: Hunts Fox Kitten's hallmark post-exploitation pattern: deploying ngrok, frpc, plink, or chisel to expose internal RDP/SSH back through an attacker-controlled relay, paired with creation of a local administrator account (often 'sysadmin', 'help', or rebranded service accounts) used to maintain access and broker ransomware affiliates (Pay2Key, NoEscape, ALPHV per CISA AA24-241A).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","chisel.exe","plink.exe","ssh.exe","revsocks.exe","gost.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*ngrok* tcp*","*ngrok*authtoken*","*-R *:*:*","*plink*-R*","*frpc*-c*","*chisel*client*","*ssh*-R*3389*","*ssh*-D*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_id host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer host [ | tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as tunnel_dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as tunnel_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (22,443,7000,4443) AND (All_Traffic.dest IN ("*.ngrok.io","*.ngrok-free.app","*.ngrok.com","*.serveo.net","*.localtunnel.me") OR match(All_Traffic.dest,"^(?:5\.42\.|45\.9\.|185\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)"))) by host All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port] | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=true count as new_account_events values(Authentication.user) as new_user from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.signature_id IN (4720,4732,4728) by host Authentication.dest] | where isnotnull(tunnel_dest) AND isnotnull(new_user) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let tunnel_bins = dynamic(["ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","frps.exe","chisel.exe","plink.exe","revsocks.exe","gost.exe","socat.exe"]);
let tunnel_cmd_re = @"(?i)(ngrok\s+(tcp|http|authtoken)|-R\s+\d+:|frpc\s+-c|chisel\s+client|ssh\s+.*-R\s+.*:3389|plink.*-R)";
let tunnel_domains = dynamic([".ngrok.io",".ngrok-free.app",".ngrok.com",".serveo.net",".localtunnel.me",".trycloudflare.com"]);
let TunnelProcs = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (tunnel_bins) or ProcessCommandLine matches regex tunnel_cmd_re
| project TunnelTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, TunnelProc=FileName, TunnelCmd=ProcessCommandLine, TunnelSHA256=SHA256;
let TunnelNet = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (tunnel_domains) or RemotePort in (4443,7000) or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (tunnel_bins)
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, NetProc=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let NewLocalAcct = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "net.exe" or FileName =~ "net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("user /add","localgroup administrators /add","localgroup \"Remote Desktop Users\" /add")
| extend NewUser = extract(@"user\s+([\w\.\-\$]+)\s+", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project AcctTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, NewUser, AcctCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
TunnelProcs
| join kind=inner TunnelNet on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', TunnelTime, NetTime)) <= 30
| join kind=leftouter NewLocalAcct on DeviceId
| where isnotnull(NewUser) or NewUser matches regex @"(?i)(sysadmin|help|support|admin\$|backup|service|temp)"
| extend FoxKittenScore = iff(isnotempty(NewUser), "HIGH - tunnel + local-acct creation", "MEDIUM - tunnel only")
| project TunnelTime, DeviceName, AccountName, TunnelProc, TunnelCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NewUser, AcctCmd, FoxKittenScore
| sort by TunnelTime desc
ucs.rationale: CrowdStrike (PIONEER KITTEN), ClearSky (Fox Kitten / Pay2Key), and CISA AA24-241A all describe Fox Kitten dropping ngrok and SSH reverse tunnels (often `ssh -R 3389`) immediately after VPN compromise to give Iranian operators and ransomware affiliates RDP back into the victim, plus creating a local privileged account named like `help`, `sysadmin`, or `IT-support` to anchor that access. Joining tun
